Petition For Return Of Property; Conversion; Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; Constructive Fraud; Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; Double Damages; And Attorney?S Fees
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Continued To Jun-01-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner Eddie Caboara, who was also present (via Zoom).
Timothy Barteau and Diego Garcia for respondent Victoria Beuerle (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Fourth Amended Petition to Set Aside Order for Final Distribution; and 2) Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary.

Mr. Barteau reported that discovery had just commenced, with additional discovery and depositions anticipated, and that a motion to bifurcate the matters was expected to be filed. He requested a one-hundred-twenty-day continuance to allow time for further developments. The Court stated that 120 days was excessive but agreed to continue the matter for ninety days instead.

The Court inquired regarding the possibility of mediation, and counsel indicated mediation may be appropriate following initial discovery. Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to June 1, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The parties were directed to file a joint status statement at least three days prior to the hearing, and the Court stated that a case management order would issue.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
